description:
Prerequisite for fz-rcp.5: publish `CallsiteOutcome::Emitted` for
`EmitSlot::Cont` (and reconsider `EmitSlot::MakeClosure` — needed
by .5's closure body picker read).

## Why this lands first

fz-rcp.5 has codegen read `module.callsite_outcomes[cid].target`
at every dispatch site. Today, only `EmitSlot::Direct`,
`ClosureLit`, and `CallClosureKnown` get outcomes published.
`EmitSlot::Cont` is explicitly skipped at ir_typer.rs:729:

```rust
EmitSlot::Cont | EmitSlot::MakeClosure(_) => {}
```

Without Cont outcomes, .5's cont-resolve read has nothing to read.
This ticket plugs the gap. Pure data plumbing — no behavior change
in isolation (codegen still resolves via spec_registry).

## What lands

- Move `EmitSlot::Cont` from the skip arm to the publish arm at
  ir_typer.rs:719. Same shape as the other variants:
  `CallsiteOutcome::Emitted { target: site.target.clone(),
  came_from: None }`.
- Decide on `EmitSlot::MakeClosure(_)`: the closure body picker
  resolves the lambda's body via captures + arg-fill. If .5 reads
  outcomes here too, MakeClosure outcomes must be published.
  Recommend: publish, mirror the same pattern.

## Acceptance

- Each `EmitSlot::Cont` and `EmitSlot::MakeClosure` emitter site
  in `produces` writes a corresponding `Emitted` outcome to
  `mt.callsite_outcome_updates`.
- `apply_callsite_outcomes` merges them into
  `module.callsite_outcomes` per existing logic.
- 710+ unit + 168 fixture-matrix tests green (no behavior
  change yet — codegen still calls resolve).
- Sets up fz-rcp.5 cleanly.

## Note from worktree investigation

The original .4 framing suggested publishing Cont outcomes alone
would retire the `.29.12.1` panic. It won't — codegen still calls
resolve, which still queries with codegen's flow-narrowed key,
which still misses the typer's narrower registered key. .4 is
data-only; .5 is what closes the panic by skipping resolve.
title: fz-rcp.4: typer publishes Emitted outcomes for EmitSlot::Cont — retire .29.12.1 panic
